source.definition: to set up a program or device and make it ready for use

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "install" comes from the Italian "installare", which itself derives from the Latin "installare", meaning "to place in position".
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „install” provine din italianul „installare”, care la rândul său derivă din latinul „installare”, însemnând „a pune într-o poziție”.

source.additional: Regular verb. Commonly used in technical contexts, especially related to software, devices, and infrastructure.

target.definition: a pregăti un program sau un dispozitiv pentru utilizare

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a instala” was borrowed from the Italian „installare”, which in turn comes from the Late Latin „installare” ("to set up, to place").
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a instala” este împrumutat din italianul „installare”, care provine la rândul său din latinul târziu „installare” („a pune, a instala”).

target.additional: Regular first conjugation verb (-a). Important in IT and technology for initializing and making software or hardware operational.

explanation.sourceText: Used in technical contexts, e.g., setting up software or devices.
explanation.targetText: Utilizare în context tehnic, de exemplu pentru configurarea software-ului sau a dispozitivelor.

examples.sourceText: I install the new software.
Do you install the program on your computer?

explanation.sourceText: Refers to setting up or assembling hardware components.
explanation.targetText: Se referă la configurarea sau montarea componentelor hardware.

examples.sourceText: The technician installs the new hardware.
I install the new software.
Do you install the program on your computer?
The technician installs the new hardware.
We will install the system tomorrow.
Are you installing the changes?
They install the devices in the office.
I have installed the update.
They have installed the software.
He often installed the new program.
I had already installed the software.
I will install the application on Friday.
She will install the updates on Saturday.
examples.targetText: Ea o să instaleze actualizările sâmbătă.

examples.sourceText: I would install the software if I had time.
examples.targetText: Aș instala software-ul dacă aș avea timp.

examples.sourceText: It is important that you install the program.
Install the new device!
Please install the changes!
The installed software works perfectly.
The update is now installed.
While installing, he placed his face in front of the monitor.
examples.targetText: Instalând, și-a pus fața în fața monitorului.

learningTips.text: The English word "install" sounds like "in stall"—imagine putting something firmly in a stall or place to remember its meaning.
Remember that "install" is related to setting something up, just like you might "install" a new app on your phone.
The Romanian verb "a instala" looks and sounds very similar to the English "install", making it easier to remember.
Think of "install" as placing something inside a system, like installing hardware inside a computer case.
